output.editContext: Post-edit QA: JS breakpoint fix, TSX/PHP nav list class sync, desktop right column always renders, ArrowDown/ArrowUp on closed submenu buttons, Home/End key support in submenus, aria-label on submenu containers, test page updated with 9 DemoContainer scenarios. Changes are primarily behavioral (JS/a11y), so visual appearance should be largely unchanged.

compositionGate.summary: The overall header composition matches the Figma design across all breakpoints. The header renders as a compact horizontal dark bar with logo on the left, search/SEP link/navigation on the right. Mobile views correctly show the SEP button above a simplified nav bar with logo and hamburger icon. All 9 test scenarios render correctly with appropriate variations.

observations: Full Content scenario: Dark (#2C2C2C) header bar approximately 90-100px tall. Logo (IGNITE with bee icon) positioned left at roughly 32px inset. Search field and 'Student Engagement Platform' link with external arrow positioned in the upper-right area. Navigation items (About, Health, Financial Wellbeing, Advocacy, Social, Student Voices) displayed horizontally below the search/SEP row, right-aligned. Chevron down icons visible on items with submenus. This matches the Figma design closely.
Minimal Content scenario: Header renders with only logo, search, and two nav items (About, Contact) without submenu chevrons. The layout adapts correctly with items right-aligned.
Hide on Scroll Down scenarios: Headers render identically to Full Content in their static state, which is correct since the hide behavior is JS-driven and not visible in a static screenshot.
Not Fixed on Scroll scenario: Header appears identical in structure; the difference is behavioral (position: static vs fixed).
Submenu Alignment Right scenario: Shows 'About' and 'Right Menu' with a chevron, layout correct.

Many Nav Items (Overflow) scenario: 8 nav items (About, Health, Financial Wellbeing, Advocacy, Social, Student Voices, Resources, Events) wrap correctly within the available space. All items are visible and readable.
Long Submenu Items scenario: Shows 'Services' with chevron, layout correct.
SEP Link Only (No Search URL) scenario: Shows search field, 'External Portal' link with arrow, and 'About' nav item. Layout correct.
desktop-1440.crossBrowserConsistency: Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit render identically at 1440px. No cross-browser discrepancies observed.

observations: Full Content scenario: Desktop nav still displays at 1024px, matching the Figma breakpoint behavior. Logo, search, SEP link, and navigation row all render correctly. The breakpoint fix (48rem -> 60rem) ensures desktop layout is maintained at this width.
Many Nav Items scenario: All 8 nav items still display horizontally at 1024px. The row wraps with 'Events' dropping to a second line, which is acceptable flex-wrap behavior for this many items.
All other scenarios render correctly with the desktop layout at this width.
desktop-1024.crossBrowserConsistency: Consistent across all three browsers at 1024px.

observations: At 768px, all scenarios correctly switch to mobile layout: SEP button displayed as a full-width dark button above the nav bar, and the nav bar shows only the logo (left) and hamburger menu icon (right). This matches the Figma mobile nav design (node 801:58305).
The SEP button shows 'Student Engagement Platform' with an external link arrow, rendered in a dark rounded-rectangle button spanning the full width. This matches the Figma design.
For scenarios without SEP link configured (Minimal, Not Fixed, Submenu Alignment Right, Many Nav Items, Long Submenu Items), only the nav bar row with logo and hamburger displays, which is correct.
The SEP Link Only scenario correctly shows the 'External Portal' button.
tablet-768.crossBrowserConsistency: Consistent across all three browsers at 768px.

observations: At 375px, all scenarios display the mobile layout correctly. The SEP button and nav bar are properly sized for mobile viewport.
The IGNITE logo scales appropriately and remains legible. The hamburger icon is properly positioned to the right.
For scenarios with SEP link, the button text and arrow icon are properly contained within the viewport width.
The overall proportions and spacing match the Figma mobile nav closed state (node 801:58305).
mobile-375.crossBrowserConsistency: Consistent across all three browsers at 375px.

layout.details: Desktop: Two-row header with logo left, search/SEP right, nav items right-aligned below. Mobile: Optional SEP button above, logo+hamburger nav bar. Layout structure matches Figma across all breakpoints.

typography.details: Navigation items use uppercase text, matching Figma. Font appears to be the correct sans-serif family. Text sizing is consistent -- nav items appear approximately 13-14px which aligns with Figma measurements (19px frame heights with padding). 'SEARCH' placeholder and 'STUDENT ENGAGEMENT PLATFORM' text both render in uppercase as expected.

colors.details: Header background is dark (#2C2C2C or similar dark gray), matching Figma. Text is white/light, providing good contrast. The IGNITE logo includes the correct green/blue/white bee icon. The SEP button on mobile uses the same dark background with white text. Active/highlighted nav items appear in the expected accent color (lime/chartreuse green visible in Figma for open states like 'ABOUT' and 'HEALTH').

spacing.details: Logo has approximately 32px left padding, matching Figma. Navigation items have consistent horizontal spacing between them (~32px gaps). The search field and SEP link are separated by a vertical divider line, matching Figma. Vertical padding within the header bar appears approximately 16-20px top/bottom, creating the ~90-100px total height that matches Figma's 98px.

borderRadius.details: No significant border-radius on the main header bar, which is correct per Figma. The mobile SEP button has rounded corners matching the Figma design. Submenu dropdowns (not visible in static screenshots) would have rounded corners per the Figma dropdown design.

alignment.details: Logo vertically centered in the header bar. Search/SEP link row right-aligned. Navigation items right-aligned below search row. On mobile, logo left-aligned, hamburger right-aligned. All alignment matches Figma reference.

proportions.details: Logo proportions (bee icon + IGNITE text) are correct and match Figma. The relative sizes of the search field, SEP link area, and navigation row are proportionally correct. Mobile nav bar height is compact, approximately 50-60px matching Figma's ~57px nav row.

scenarioCoverage.fullContent: PASS - All features visible: logo, search, SEP link, nav items with submenu chevrons
scenarioCoverage.minimalContent: PASS - Only logo, search, and 2 plain nav items. No SEP, no submenus. Correct empty-state handling.
scenarioCoverage.hideOnScrollDownBoth: PASS - Static render matches; scroll behavior is JS/behavioral, not visual QA scope
scenarioCoverage.hideOnScrollDownDesktopOnly: PASS - Static render matches; behavioral difference only
scenarioCoverage.notFixedOnScroll: PASS - Static render matches; position difference is behavioral
scenarioCoverage.submenuAlignmentRight: PASS - Layout correct with 'Right Menu' showing chevron for submenu
scenarioCoverage.manyNavItems: PASS - 8 items wrap correctly at desktop widths; all readable
scenarioCoverage.sepLinkOnly: PASS - External Portal link with arrow displays correctly alongside search and nav

notes.description: The changes under QA (JS breakpoint fix, TSX/PHP class sync, desktop right column rendering, keyboard navigation, aria-labels) are all behavioral/accessibility changes. No visual regressions were introduced. The header appearance is consistent with the Figma design across all breakpoints and browsers.

Submenu open states and mobile drawer open states cannot be verified in static full-page screenshots since they require JavaScript interaction. The Figma reference shows these states (dropdown menus, mobile drawer with accordion navigation), but testing them visually would require interactive screenshots with click actions. The Functional QA agent covers interactive behavior.

The 'Many Nav Items' scenario at 1024px shows 'Events' wrapping to a second line, which is expected flex-wrap behavior when many items exceed the available row width. This is acceptable responsive behavior.
